_publications._publicationTitle: Global proteomics and affinity mass spectrometry analysis of human Schwann cells indicates that variation in and loss of neurofibromin (NF1) alters protein expression and cellular and mitochondrial metabolism.

_contextSnippet: NF1 -/- cells displayed a ~ 30% reduction in OXPHOS and electron transfer capacity associated with convergent flow of NADH-linked substrates and succinate
details: NF1 knockout cells displayed ~30% reduction in OXPHOS and electron transfer capacity with convergent substrate flow
